# SWAMN

> SWAMN builds autonomous, AI-assisted systems that detect and collect floating plastic waste from oceans, rivers, and water bodies.

SWAMN is a student-led environmental engineering initiative from Sunbeam School, Mughalsarai (Uttar Pradesh, India). The team designs autonomous surface bots and smart docking stations that use computer vision and AI-driven navigation to clean rivers, lakes, and coastal waters. SWAMN aligns its work with UN Sustainable Development Goals 14 (Life Below Water), 12 (Responsible Consumption), and 7 (Affordable Clean Energy).

## Pages
- [Home](/): Mission, technology, team, impact, and roadmap.
- [Updates](/updates): Blog posts, milestones, and field notes.
- [Sponsors & Partners](/sponsors): Sponsorship tiers and how to support SWAMN.
- [Press Kit](/press): Brand assets, boilerplate, and press contact.
- [Join the Mission](/join): Contact form for partnerships, sponsorships, and team applications.

## Topics
- Autonomous surface vehicles for water cleanup
- AI-assisted plastic detection and navigation
- Solar-powered, modular docking stations
- Student-led environmental engineering
